task_context.actionable_question: Am I using the full range of learning resources available?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Use vle_diversity_score [FE] to show breadth. Highlight under-used types (e.g. forum rarely used).

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: Your engagement with various learning resources shows a strong preference for quizzes, which is great! However, there are several resources that you could explore more to enhance your learning experience.

Insights: High Quiz Engagement: You have clicked on quizzes 623 times, making up nearly 49% of your total resource usage. This indicates a strong engagement with this resource type. | Low Forum Engagement: Your engagement with forums is quite low, with only 140 clicks, which is about 11% of your total usage. This suggests you might be missing out on valuable discussions and peer interactions. | Underutilized Resources: Other resources like 'subpage' and 'resource' have very low engagement, with only 94 and 71 clicks respectively. Exploring these could provide additional insights and support.

Educational implications: Engaging with a wider variety of resources can deepen your understanding and retention of course material.

Recommendations: high - Increase your participation in forums. - This will help you connect with peers and gain different perspectives on the material. | medium - Explore underutilized resources like subpages and additional materials. - These resources may provide valuable information that complements your learning.

explanation.summary: Your engagement with various learning resources shows a strong preference for quizzes, which is great! However, there are several resources that you could explore more to enhance your learning experience.

insights.title: High Quiz Engagement
insights.description: You have clicked on quizzes 623 times, making up nearly 49% of your total resource usage. This indicates a strong engagement with this resource type.

evidence.context: Quizzes are your most engaged resource.

insights.title: Low Forum Engagement
insights.description: Your engagement with forums is quite low, with only 140 clicks, which is about 11% of your total usage. This suggests you might be missing out on valuable discussions and peer interactions.

evidence.context: Forums can enhance your understanding through peer discussions.

insights.title: Underutilized Resources
insights.description: Other resources like 'subpage' and 'resource' have very low engagement, with only 94 and 71 clicks respectively. Exploring these could provide additional insights and support.

evidence.context: These resources may contain important supplementary materials.
Engaging with these could enhance your learning.

educational_implications: Engaging with a wider variety of resources can deepen your understanding and retention of course material.

recommendations.rationale: This will help you connect with peers and gain different perspectives on the material.

recommendations.action: Explore underutilized resources like subpages and additional materials.
recommendations.rationale: These resources may provide valuable information that complements your learning.

confidence.reason: The data is complete and provides a clear picture of your resource engagement.

required_core_outputs.description: State the student's resource usage breadth across available VLE types.
Identify under-used resource types when a comparison baseline is available.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use vle_diversity_score as the primary resource-breadth metric when returned.
Do not label low usage of a resource type as disengagement when no comparison baseline is provided.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.
